## Summary

Adds a formal audit document for the OpenAI API cost spike after two evaluations reportedly cost about $64 USD.

This documents why PR #937 was a partial stop-loss rather than proof of production optimization.

## Key finding

PR #937 made cheaper routing possible, but it did not prove cheaper routing is active in production because:

- the savings depend on Vercel Production env var `EVAL_CHEAP_MODEL=gpt-5-mini`;
- `evaluationRuntimeConfig.ts` diagnostics still do not include `EVAL_CHEAP_MODEL` in Pass 1 / Pass 2 routing;
- cost telemetry appears not fully wired into every OpenAI call path;
- legacy OpenAI entry points still require audit;
- no hard `gpt-5.5` escalation guard exists yet.

## Included

- Required code changes
- Required regression tests
- Production env checklist
- GPT-5.5 guard requirements
- Per-call telemetry requirements
- Cost ceiling recommendation
- Acceptance criteria before anyone can say the issue is solved

## Follow-up required

This PR is an audit marker, not the full behavioral patch. The next PR should patch `evaluationRuntimeConfig.ts`, add tests, and wire telemetry/guards.
